channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk's Role in Foreign Politics Raises Questions for U.S. Allies
item.description: Elon Musk's increasing involvement in foreign politics through his social platform X has raised questions among U.S. allies and created frustration within the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: AMD and Nvidia could be set for epic GPU showdown with RX 9070 and 9070 XT going on sale at the same time as the RTX 5080
item.description: AMD RX 9070 and 9070 XT graphics cards might be here sooner than you think – pre-orders could go live on January 23.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Estas son las prioridades de inversión en ‘tech’ para 2025
item.description: La inversión en TI está experimentando un interesante incremento. Las previsiones de la firma de análisis Gartner apuntan a un crecimiento en Europa del dinero destinado por las organizaciones a esta partida del 8,7% en 2025.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Chief data officers step into the business strategist spotlight
item.description: With data central to every aspect of business, the chief data officer has become a highly strategic executive. Today’s CDO is focused on helping the organization leverage data as a business asset to drive outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 9 buoni propositi IT per il 2025
item.description: I CIO sono molto ambiziosi. Per l’anno appena iniziato, non sembrano volersi accontentare dello status quo e, al contrario, fissano grandi obiettivi, secondo innumerevoli sondaggi realizzati di recente sui dirigenti IT.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta tests letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings in a bid to appease the EU
item.description: Meta is testing a feature that will let Facebook users browse eBay listings on Facebook Marketplace before being redirected to complete the purchase on eBay. The test, which is being launched to a small group in Germany, France, and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta tests eBay listings in Facebook Marketplace to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok alternative Lemon8: Why creators are moving to ByteDance’s other social media platform
item.description: “Okay it’s happening… are we switching to Lemon8 or what’s the plan?” posted one TikTok user back in December. With a potential U.S. ban just days away, content creators scrambling for an alternative are turning their attention to the platform’s sister app, Lemon8.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Competencia lanza un contrato para detectar mediante IA licitaciones irregulares
item.description: La Comisión Nacional de los Mercados y la Competencia (CNMC) ha lanzado un contrato pionero para detectar mediante algoritmos de inteligencia artificial (IA) posibles prácticas fraudulentas en licitaciones públicas. Así lo ha adelantado elDiario.es.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Monster Hunter Wilds is getting a second beta test in February, and you won't need PS Plus or Game Pass to take part
item.description: A second Monster Hunter Wilds beta test is happening in February - here's everything you need to know.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Jeff Bezos’ Amazon pays $40 million for Melania Trump documentary
item.description: The choice by Bezos's film and TV studio to pay such a sum to produce Melania Trump's 'vanity documentary' represents the latest public effort by the Amazon founder to "warm up" to Trump after years of feuding, media observers say.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Man used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas Cybertruck blast
item.description: The man killed in the Las Vegas Cybertruck blast used ChatGPT to plan the explosion, police said. In a press conference, Tuesday, Las Vegas police released more details of the intentions of 37-year-old Matthew Livelsberger, who died of a gunshot wound prior to the car exploding.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security
item.description: The global IT skills shortage is predicted to impact 90% of organizations by 2026, according to IDC. The post Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI is losing money on its pricey ChatGPT Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, in a post on X, says the AI ​​company is currently losing money on its ChatGPT Pro subscription. “People are using it much more than we expected,” he wrote. The company introduced its ChatGPT Pro subscription in December.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mistakes Happen: Apple's AI Error and the Future of News
item.description: Apple's AI makes mistakes, just like people do. The implications are huge for news and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI's new warning about AI-driven scams that are after your cash
item.description: FBI warns that criminals are using generative AI to exploit individuals with deceptive tactics. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains their tactics and how to protect yourself from them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WordPress.org Services Shutdown: Uncertainty Surrounds Open Source Project - News Article
item.description: WordPress.org staff announced the shutdown of services, increasing uncertainty surrounding the open source project. Enterprise users may reconsider using WordPress due to concerns about the software's reliability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Linux filesystems: Ext4, Btrfs, XFS, ZFS and more
item.description: Linux supports quite a few filesystem types. Your choice of which filesystem to use can play an important role in optimizing performance, reliability and data integrity. Among the more popular filesystems are Ext4, Btrfs, XFS and ZFS. Each is widely used and offers some unique features and benefits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk stays on brand following Tesla Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Elon Musk used his X account to defend the durability of Tesla's Cybertruck after the New Year's Las Vegas explosion.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4
